title: Change Check Overlap
description: Users need a way to check whether their in-progress work overlaps with other active changes targeting the same specs. Without a dedicated command, overlap is invisible until archive time when delta application fails. `specd change check-overlap` surfaces this information proactively.
context: Users need a way to check whether their in-progress work overlaps with other active changes targeting the same specs. Without a dedicated command, overlap is invisible until archive time when delta application fails. `specd change check-overlap` surfaces this information proactively.

rules:
```text
specd change check-overlap [<name>] [--format text|json|toon]
```

- `<name>` — optional; when provided, shows overlap only for the named change
- `--format text|json|toon` — optional; output format, defaults to `text`

In `text` mode, the command MUST print a grouped display where each overlapping spec is a header followed by the changes that target it:

```text
<specId>
  <changeName>  <state>
  <changeName>  <state>

<specId>
  <changeName>  <state>
  <changeName>  <state>
```

Spec IDs MUST be sorted lexicographically. Changes within each group MUST be sorted by name.

When no overlap is detected, the command MUST:

- In `text` mode: print `no overlap detected` to stdout
- In `json`/`toon` mode: output `{"hasOverlap":false,"entries":[]}`

The process MUST exit with code 0.
When `<name>` is provided but no active change with that name exists, the command MUST print an error message to stderr and exit with code 1.

- `0` — command succeeded (overlap or no overlap)
- `1` — named change not found or unexpected error

Overlap presence MUST NOT cause a non-zero exit code.

constraints: The command MUST delegate to DetectOverlap via the kernel — it MUST NOT scan changes directly
No filtering by workspace or spec ID in v1

scenarios.name: Command accepts optional name and format
when: specd change check-overlap is run without arguments
then: it shows overlap for all active changes

scenarios.name: Named change filter
when: specd change check-overlap my-change is run
then: the output only shows overlap entries involving my-change

scenarios.name: Two specs overlapping across multiple changes
given: changes alpha (designing) and beta (implementing) both target core:core/config
AND changes beta and gamma (designing) both target core:core/kernel
when: specd change check-overlap is run in text mode
then: stdout shows two groups, sorted by spec ID
AND the first group header is core:core/config with alpha and beta listed below
AND the second group header is core:core/kernel with beta and gamma listed below

scenarios.name: JSON output matches OverlapReport structure
given: overlap exists between two changes on one spec
when: specd change check-overlap --format json is run
then: stdout is valid JSON with hasOverlap equal to true
AND entries is an array of objects with specId and changes fields

scenarios.name: No overlap in text mode
given: no specs are targeted by more than one active change

scenarios.name: No overlap in JSON mode
when: specd change check-overlap --format json is run

scenarios.name: Error for nonexistent change
when: specd change check-overlap nonexistent is run

scenarios.name: Overlap present does not affect exit code
